user_query:
I’m putting together a preview piece for the Fitchburg Red Raiders’ next game and prepping our newsroom plan. Using only the files under input/, please do the following and produce the three deliverables under output/:

Inputs:
- input/stats/fitchburg_box_scores.csv
- input/roster.json
- input/schedule.html
- input/editor_notes.md

Deliverables:
1) output/top_performers.csv
- Purpose: a ranked list of the top 5 Red Raiders by total touchdowns across all games in input/stats/fitchburg_box_scores.csv.
- Required fields (in this order): rank,player,number,position,class,games_played,total_touchdowns,total_yards
- Definitions:
  - games_played = number of rows for the player in the CSV.
  - total_touchdowns = td_rush + td_rec + td_pass summed across games.
  - total_yards = rushing_yards + receiving_yards + passing_yards summed across games.
  - class must come from input/roster.json (join on player name); if a player in the CSV isn’t in the roster, exclude them from the ranking.
- Sorting and ranking: sort by total_touchdowns (desc), then total_yards (desc), then player name (asc). Assign rank starting at 1 after sorting and include only the top 5 rows.

2) output/preview_article.md
- Purpose: a short, publishable preview article draft for the next game.
- Use input/schedule.html to identify the next opponent: select the row with data-status="next" and extract Date, Opponent, and Location. This opponent must be the focus of the piece.
- Use the computed ranking from output/top_performers.csv to inform the writeup.
- Content requirements:
  - Headline that includes “Fitchburg vs {Opponent} Preview”.
  - Dek (one-sentence subhead) that mentions the Location (Home/Away) and the Date from schedule.html.
  - Three short paragraphs:
    1) Set up the matchup with the opponent and location, grounded in schedule.html.
    2) Highlight at least the top-ranked player from output/top_performers.csv by name with his total_touchdowns and total_yards, and mention his class (from roster.json) and number. Also briefly reference at least one other ranked player.
    3) What to watch: include one focus point pulled from input/editor_notes.md under the “Focus:” section. If the opponent is “Nashoba,” explicitly mention the rivalry angle.

3) output/meeting_notes.md
- Purpose: prep notes and clear action items for the newsroom.
- Sections and content:
  - “Meeting Notes” section that:
    - States the next opponent, date, and location extracted from input/schedule.html.
    - Lists the top 3 players from output/top_performers.csv as bullet points with: player, number, class, total_touchdowns, total_yards.
  - “Action Items” section that includes:
    - Every line from input/editor_notes.md that begins with “ACTION:” as individual bullets (preserve the text).
    - Auto-generated items based on extracted data:
      - “Request photo coverage for next game vs {Opponent} ({Location}).”
      - “Photo assignments: capture top 3 — {Player 1} (#{number}, {class}), {Player 2} (#{number}, {class}), {Player 3} (#{number}, {class}).”
      - If a single “Coach:” line exists in input/editor_notes.md, add: “Call {Coach Name} for a pregame quote.”

Notes and constraints:
- Read and extract all relevant content only from the provided files. Do not assume any external context or use the Internet.
- Make the extraction from input/schedule.html structural (use the row with data-status="next").
- The article and meeting notes must be consistent with the computed top_performers.csv and the provided inputs.
- Ensure all three deliverables are created at the specified output/ paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
